name: I'm pro se now - can I see AEO documents?
description: **Motion to modify protective order** - letter first, then court with declaration.[3]

mainEntity.name: I'm pro se now - can I see AEO documents?

acceptedAnswer.text: **Motion to modify protective order** - letter first, then court with declaration.[3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**As pro se, you likely need a court motion to access AEO docs, depending on protective order language.**[3]

Orders often limit to licensed attorneys; pro se status changes this, but courts may modify with use restrictions.[3]

- **Start with letter**: Request access explaining trial prep need.[3]
- **If denied, motion**: Include declaration, their refusal.[3]
- **Limits apply**: No public sharing.[3]

Send polite letter to counsel, then file motion if refused. Avoid self-help breaches.
